prompt:
You are shown one page from a SEC 10-K filing rendered as an image.
This page contains the BEGINNING of "Item 1. Business".
Find the "Item 1" heading, then transcribe VERBATIM the first 5 lines of text that come immediately after it. Include any report title, addressee, or sub-heading exactly as printed — do NOT skip them; only ignore page running-headers, page numbers, and footers.
Output ONLY the transcribed text, no commentary, no quotes, no formatting.
text:
This page shows the index/table of contents, not the beginning of Item 1. Business itself. I need to find the actual Item 1. Business heading and the text that follows it.

Based on the index shown, Item 1. Business appears on page 4. Since this image only shows the index page (page with the table of contents), I cannot see the actual Item 1. Business content from this image alone.

However, if you're asking me to identify what comes after "Item 1." in this index page itself, that would be:

Item 1. Business

But that's just the index entry, not the beginning of the actual Item 1. Business section.

If you have the actual page where Item 1. Business begins (which would be page 4 according to this index), please provide that image and I can transcribe the first 5 lines after the Item 1. Business heading.

If you're asking me to work with only what's visible in this image, then after "Item 1." the text is simply "Business" (as a hyperlink in the index). The first 5 lines of actual content after the "Item 1." heading in this index would be:

Business
Item 1A. Risk Factors
Item 1B. Unresolved Staff Comments
Item 1C. Cybersecurity
Item 2. Properties

But I believe you may be looking for the actual content of Item 1. Business from page 4 of the filing, which is not shown in this image.
